danger_signals.visual_form: Muzzle flashes, beam effects, projectile trajectories
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat engagement; enemy forces engaging player units

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive visual degradation, smoke/fire effects on structures
danger_signals.meaning: Structural vulnerability; buildings under siege or damaged

danger_signals.visual_form: Organic purple-black growths, creep spreading across terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Zerg contamination; territory claimed by hostile forces
Loss of military strength; unit eliminated

danger_signals.visual_form: Red dots for hostile units, changing density/clustering
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy force concentration; imminent attack vectors

danger_signals.visual_form: Alarm sounds paired with visual warning flash
danger_signals.meaning: Unit under attack; immediate danger to player units

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-colored terrain tint and owned structures
Units with matching color team identifier clustering
Fully constructed building, no damage indicators
Fully revealed and lit terrain areas
safety_signals.meaning: Scouted territory; no hidden threats detected

safety_signals.visual_form: Mineral formations and vespene geysers with clear visual models

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy Terran units use opposing color (blue, purple, yellow) with same mechanical silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Metal structures, mechanical architecture, angular building designs, tactical square bases

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Organic carapace design; insectoid imagery; swarm iconography
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Purple-tinted chitinous units; infected/mutated appearance when assimilating other species
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Creep carpet (organic purple ground texture), hive-like curved structures, biomass-based buildings, evolving/growing aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crystalline/geometric patterns; psionic rune motifs; sleek angular design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy Protoss use color-shifted variants while maintaining crystalline silhouettes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crystalline structures, floating/hovering buildings, glowing energy conduits, psionic aura effects, elegant curved architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Supreme creator beings; godlike visual presentation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears primarily in cinematics; not playable as faction in base game
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ancient technology; dimensional rifts in cutscenes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minimal unit count on map; scattered buildings; fog of war dominates screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units move slowly in small groups; workers focus on resource gathering; minimal combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple faction bases visible; terrain heavily claimed with building clusters; creep spread increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased unit production; scouting raids; skirmish-level combat at map chokepoints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large unit balls visible on map; multiple explosion effects; minimap saturated with color indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Macro-level unit movement; organized assault formations; constant combat animation feedback

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Purple creep carpet gradually consuming neutral terrain; Zerg structures emerging from ground; visual corruption overlay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zerg units spawn faster on creep; Protoss/Terran units move slower on creep; map loses neutral aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units surrounding player base perimeter; smoke effects intensify; building health bars visible in red
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defending units cluster at gates/chokepoints; defensive structures fire continuously; worker units cease economic activity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unit models upgrade visually (armor plating appears, weapons become larger/more advanced); building visual complexity increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Upgraded units attack with enhanced animations; new unit types produce from buildings; tactical options expand

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Base buildings destroyed; unit count on minimap drastically reduced; player color territory shrinks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining units desperate; workers flee; production halts; final stand formations at remaining structures

environmental_storytelling.example: Terran bases immediately recognizable by industrial metal framework; Protoss by crystalline floating platforms; Zerg by organic hive structures—teaches player to identify control zones instantly
Purple creep spreading across terrain visually communicates Zerg presence and infestation progression; non-player can 'read' territorial conquest without UI
Mechanical Terran soldiers vs. chitinous Zerg drones vs. crystalline Protoss units instantly differentiate by shape; enables rapid threat identification in large battles
Buildings showing fire, smoke, and structural cracks communicate ongoing conflict and vulnerability without text; players instinctively prioritize defending damaged structures
Up to 8 player colors in multiplayer (Red, Blue, Teal, Purple, Yellow, Brown, White, Orange) allow instant ally/enemy recognition; color persistence in unit, building, and minimap representations
Clustering of enemy colors on minimap instantly communicates attacking army concentration; empty minimap suggests exploration safety
Darkened unexplored terrain creates psychological pressure; player instinctively scouts dark areas; reveals enemy position through visual disclosure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stance animation; weapon at ready; scanning movement; clear alertness
Attack animation triggers; muzzle flash; recoil effect; unit vocalizes
Directional movement animation; weapon pointed forward; speed increases toward target
Chittering animation; aggressive posture; periodic pounce preparation movement
Rapid movement animation; leaping gait; pounce attack animation on contact
Hatchery opens; organic spawn animation; new unit emerges from structure; creep pulses with spawn event
Unit positions near building site; glowing beam projects from unit to construction; psionic energy visualization
Sprint animation; psionic energy crackles around unit; shield visualization intensifies
Partial structural model; construction beam animation (worker projecting energy); progress overlay indicator
Full model renders; glow aura activates; defensive animations loop (gun turret rotation, shield flickers, etc.)
Repeated harvest animation loop; resource indicator above head; movement between mineral/geyser and base
Approach building site; beam projection; reduced movement speed; continued animation until structure completion

notes: StarCraft (1998) employs a visual language built on three core principles: (1) **Faction silhouette hierarchy** — each faction's units, buildings, and effects are immediately recognizable by shape, color, and animation style; (2) **State-driven visual feedback** — every unit and structure communicates its status through animation, damage effects, and behavioral tells; (3) **Territorial visual expression** — ownership is communicated through building placement, color identity, and environmental modification (especially Zerg creep). The RTS paradigm requires rapid cognitive processing of large battlefields; StarCraft achieves this through consistent visual encoding rather than explicit UI indicators. The minimap serves as a secondary 'at a glance' world-state translator. Cinematics (integrated narrative element unique for 1998 RTS) provide political context that the in-game world-state visuals then reinforce through faction-specific unit behavior and environmental corruption patterns.
